title: BCA Reports Rp 25 Trillion in Net Profit Through May 2025, Driven by Dividends and Cost Efficiency
body: Even without factoring in dividends, BCA’s net profit still posted a solid 10.12 percent year-on-year increase.

title: Indonesia Lifts Quota on Live Cattle Imports to Boost Meat and Dairy Supply
body: Indonesia plans to import up to 2 million live cattle over the next five years, including 1.2 million dedicated to dairy production.

title: Qatar’s Barzan Doubts ‘Big Market’ Indonesia Will Put Brake on Its Defense Investment
body: According to Barzan Holdings, Indonesia's defense investment will remain unaffected by economic hurdles.

title: Indonesia Says Domestic Output Can Cushion Oil Price Shock After Israeli Strike on Iran
body: Indonesia says rising domestic oil output helps cushion global shock after Israel’s strike on Iran sends crude prices surging over 7%.

title: Indonesia to Export Clean Electricity to Singapore Under Landmark Green Energy Deal
body: Indonesia aims to export up to 3 gigawatts of electricity under the deal.

title: Indonesia Prepares New Poverty Line Formula to Align with Global Standards
body: In April, the World Bank’s Macro Poverty Outlook reported that 60.3 percent of Indonesians lived below the poverty line in 2024.

title: Pertamina Posts $3.1 Billion in Net Profit for 2024, Announces New Board Members
body: Pertamina supplied 69 percent of the country’s oil and 37 percent of its gas needs in 2024.

title: Sri Mulyani: Government Can Only Fund 40% of $625B Infrastructure Needs
body: “This gap will require private sector participation, strong partnerships, and innovative financing strategies,” she said.

title: Achmad Ardianto Appointed as New President Director of Antam
body: Achmad brings with him an extensive background in state-owned enterprises and human capital leadership.

title: China-Led Investments to Offset Indonesia’s Recent Job Cuts, Luhut Says
body: Luhut says 67,000 new jobs could open by end-2025, fueled by Chinese-led factory projects in small towns despite current mass layoffs.

title: Qantas Closes Jetstar Asia to Fund A$500m Fleet Renewal
body: Qantas will shut Jetstar Asia by July 31, freeing A$500m to renew its fleet and expand core operations in Australia and New Zealand.

title: Nusantara to Complete 40-Meter-Wide Main Road in Six Months
body: The six-month timeline aligns with the government’s 2025 budget schedule, as the funding is sourced from the current fiscal year.

title: Indonesia Opens $12.3 Billion in Infrastructure Projects to Global Investors
body: A total of 46 projects are being offered for investment, including major toll road developments.

title: Trump Says Tariffs on Chinese Goods Will Total 55%
body: The US will provide China “what was agreed to,” including allowing Chinese students to attend American colleges and universities.

title: Indonesia’s Car Sales Drop 15% in May Amid Weak Consumer Demand
body: The surge of Chinese carmakers to Indonesia’s top ten car producers indicates a market transformation marked by growing EV demands.

title: No Indonesian Will Live in Poverty By 2045, Prabowo Says
body: Indonesia will celebrate its centennial in 2045, and Prabowo doesn't want anyone in the country to live in poverty by then.

title: New OJK Rule Requires Patients to Share Medical Bills
body: Starting Jan 1, 2026, new health insurance policies in Indonesia will require patients to pay at least 10% of medical costs as co-payment.

title: Middle-Class Businesses Overlooked, HIPMI Warns on Entrepreneurship Day
body: HIPMI urges gov’t to support Indonesia’s middle-class entrepreneurs, citing imbalance in policy focus and untapped growth potential.

title: Gag Nikel Stays, Others Go: Government Moves on Raja Ampat Mining Row
body: Bahlil presented aerial photos and videos taken during a recent site visit to Gag Island to dismiss environmental concerns in Raja Ampat.

title: Prabowo’s $1.5 Billion Economic Stimuli: Are They Good Enough?
body: Analysts sayPrabowo's latest economic stimuli the middle class, and is only a short-term strategy to boost consumption.

title: Dutch Government Sets Aside $300 Million to Back Key Indonesian Programs
body: An envoy informed that 120 Dutch companies are scheduled to visit Indonesia on a trade and investment mission on June 16.

title: Indonesia Revokes Mining Permits of Four Companies in Raja Ampat
body: The move comes after sustained pressure from environmental activists who have voiced opposition to industrial activity in Raja Ampat.

title: Danantara: No Official Discussion on GoTo Investment Yet
body: Neither GoTo nor Grab has publicly confirmed the potential merger, which has become a major topic of speculation in Indonesia.

title: Dharma Satya Sets Rp800b Capex for Palm Oil Replanting, Renewable Energy
body: DSNG sets Rp800b 2025 capex for palm oil replanting, green energy, and upgrade. Company books Rp367b Q1 profit on strong CPO prices.

title: China Says Its Exports to the US Fell 35% in May
body: Exports will likely rebound somewhat in June thanks to a 90-day suspension of most of the tariffs China and the US imposed on each other.

title: Indonesia Ignores Its Middle Class in Latest Economic Stimuli: Analyst
body: The government has recently launched economic stimuli, but these packages mainly target the low-income households, analyst says.

title: Indonesia Denies EU’s Palm Oil Law Is Reason Why Trade Pact Takes Years

title: Landmark Indonesia-EU Trade Deal Concluded, Opening Path for Greater Market Access
body: Within one to two years of CEPA implementation, 80 percent of Indonesia’s exports to the EU will enjoy zero tariffs.

title: Danantara in Talks to Join Grab’s $7B GoTo Deal: Bloomberg
body: Indonesia’s Danantara may join Grab’s $7B GoTo deal, easing regulatory concerns over foreign control of a national tech giant.

title: No Feud Here: Maruarar Says Hashim Dispute Overblown

body: Nickel Institute urges Indonesia’s nickel miners to adopt ESG standards to meet rising global demand and secure sustainable growth.

title: Indonesia to Stop Corn Imports by 2026, Prabowo Says
body: President Prabowo aims to halt corn imports by 2026, citing a 48% surge in local output and a push for farmer welfare and food independence.

title: Danantara Says Ray Dalio Stays as Unpaid Advisor
body: American investor Ray Dalio will share his views with Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara for free.

title: Indonesia Seeks to Attain Full OECD Membership in Four Years
body: The OECD represents about 75 percent of global trade and is a major source of foreign direct investment (FDI).

title: Indonesia Sees Trade Deficit with China Double, Rejects Link to US Tariffs
body: The trade minister said there is no evidence indicating that Chinese exports intended for the US market are being redirected to Indonesia.

title: Gov’t Lifts Hotel Meeting Restrictions as Industry Faces Financial Crunch
body: The ban was blamed for worsening financial strain on hotels that depend heavily on government bookings.

title: Indonesia Waives Import Duties on International Trophies and Awards
body: The exemption excludes goods on the negative import list, including motor vehicles, alcoholic beverages, and prizes won from lotteries.
